channel.title: Comments for page "Canon Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Canon Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your canon cannot be "finished."

Does this mean that a canon cannot be a story that can at some point be concluded (i.e. a canon should be a setting, rather than a storyline), or just that the storyline cannot be concluded as of the time of posting (i.e. a canon can be considered "complete" at some point, just not at the time of posting)?

If it's the former, then there are a few canons that should probably get relabeled, as the entire point of them is to advance a single main storyline, with any non-main storyline entries being relegated to at best auxiliary status (off the top of my head, Rezz, Competitive Eschatology [love you, moose <3]).

If it's the latter, and closed or completed storyline-based canons are taken off the page, then that seems like a defacto ban on storyline-based canons, just with extra steps ("This can be on the page, but once it's completed, then you have to take it off").

If it's the latter and we keep a canon with a completed storyline on the canon page, then what's the point of having the rule about finished canons (or closed canons, for that matter) in the first place?

I'm not advocating for one position or another, but I do think that there is a need for clarity. Part of the issue, imo, was putting the technical cart before the narrative horse. We know exactly how many articles are needed for a canon, but so far as I can tell, there is no clear distinction between a canon and a qualifying series beyond "one's a canon and the other isn't," or even what a canon is/should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Permission to put the canons in alphabetical order? It’s sorta hard to find what I’m looking for by name with the chronological sorting method. If I can’t do it, could I suggest that someone does?

EDIT: With permission from A Random Day, I have not only alphabetized the hub, but also added a table of contents, cleaned up some spaghetti code, and added some stolen spiff from the Groups of Interest page. Thanks for letting me do this, and hopefully this is a lot more user friendly now!

EDIT2: Also, thank you to Rounderhouse for fixing a mistake I made in regards to AIAD and Aces & Eights. Turns out some alphabetizers put capital letters as being earlier in the alphabet than lowercase letters… whoops!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was aware of the last two things (don't add to this list without a hub, not needing a tag for a hub), but I was just unsure if I could have a canon tag without a hub. I suppose that means that I will go apply for a canon tag, and make the hub at a later time. Thank you Magnus!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, if you mean "a shared universe for writing a particular set of stories."

You shouldn't add it to this list without it having a hub (as there's no way to link to it all in this place). Also, you don't need a tag to create a hub for something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can a canon exist before it has a hub? Meaning, tagged?

item.title: Re: Canon Requirements
item.content:encoded:
As of today, the Canon requirements have been relaxed by staff consensus.
The writing requirements are now five articles (SCPs, Tales, GOI Formats) by three authors.

item.title: Canon Requirements
item.content:encoded: Does it have to be five Tales? If SCPs can be tagged as part of a Canon, shouldn't five articles in general be enough? On a similar note, whatever happened in regards to the tag situation up above?

item.title: Re: Missing Tags
item.content:encoded: I believe that's because the only canons from the contest that got tags were the ones with tales written for them after the contest. Excepting Unfounded and Doctors of the Church, all of the above from the contest haven't had anything written for them. Can't give an answer for the latter two.

item.title: Missing Tags
item.content:encoded:
Looking through this, I've realised that some canons do not have their tags. Those are Unfounded, Doctors of the Church, Only Game in Town, The Man Who Wasn't There, Old Man in the Sea, Stealing Solidarity, and Global Occult Coalition Casefiles.1

Anyway, I don't see any strong reason why those won't have their own tags when the remaining canons (including the newer canons) have tags.

Footnotes
1. Okay, there is a "global-occult-coalition" tag technically. But that should be the GoI tag and not a canon tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a reason why there is a line between all the other cannons, but not between S&C Plastics and lolFoundation? Are they related in some way that I am not aware of? Just wondering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of these are canon. They're just different canons.

The site is a fluid multi-canon. That's all there is to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so, Unfounded won? Does that make it canon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the general rule is that if it doesn't suck and/or make people mad, we keep it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think there are any rules regarding mixing canons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are there any rules about (logically) mixing two canons within a tale, or would it be best if they keep separated?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then, no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because that's how people order things. It's like numerical order, but for letters!

Also, because that's the way the tales are arranged, and, obviously, it makes it easier to look up a specific entry. I can see the logic in putting the best ones at the top, but I don't agree with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given that the current order reflects the placement in the 2013 New Year's Contest (which effected the creation of the Canon Hub page), I'd say that it's fine as-is. Also, seconding Bright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it cool if I chuck these into alphabetical order?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only if you want it to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was that a pun?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is now an entry for S & C Plastics up, seeing as we now have 3 authors in the canon.

Write on!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have added Et Tam Deum Petivi, as it now has stories from three authors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …What is the difference between "check out this thing that's close to being a canon" and "check out this thing that's not quite a canon"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I meant it as "here's stuff that's close," not "check out this awesome not-quite-canon."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, on the basis that it'd get rid of the point of having a minimum anything if you can just put it on in a different place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would be the point of having minimum author/contributions then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe you could put the nearly-qualifying stuff on a post or a collapsible or somesuch?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The worst part is I've had a Site 87 tale in my sandbox for ages, but I can't seem to really work a hook into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but djoric i needs to studies…

Soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if we form a cartel to get canons that aren't on the hub yet on the hub.

e: to ride on Ihpkmn's statement, Et Tam Deum Petivi, y'all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We literally need one more author to write for S & C Plastics for it to properly qualify. That's it.

Those of you who want to write about Site 87, get to it, now. The S & C Plastics Hub is right here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't feel too bad, Ihpkmn. None of my stuff qualifies either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

A minimum of three authors writing the tales.

Mother puss bucket.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess you just better get writing, Jukse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's really neat to have all of them wrapped up in a single page, indeed. I believe that ETDP deserves to be mentioned too, despite the fact that it still hasn't met (yet) the "Three different authors" requirement, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks pretty darn good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you'd like to talk with the progenitor (Bright) and the other authors involved, I have no problems with this becoming a HUB. It's more of a running theme though, but I'd definitely take it under consideration since it "peaked" with Annon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hesitant to include it because I wasn't sure if it was headed toward becoming "closed." The hallmark of these is that people can still write in them… well… theoretically forever. If GameDay meets this qualification, and someone is willing to put in the extreme amount of work it takes to make a HUB for something of Gameday's size, then I'll be happy to add it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm hesitant to do so without permission, especially since one of my own tales is involved, but I'd love to see the Conversations with Everett Mann (or whatever you want to call it) wrapped up as a canon here. Specifically:
SCP-001:O5
Conviction
Change
Ecce Perago
Annon
Origin of Clef

Sound good?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about Game day? It's the biggest canon so far…
